$schema_note: Canonical machine-readable syllabus for exam C1000-179. This file is the root build input for the whole platform (coverage map, question generation targets, mock-exam stratification, flashcards). Provenance and confidence are tracked per field group. Generated 2026-07-19.

exam.title: Fundamentals of Quantum Computing Using Qiskit v2.X Developer
exam.badge_title: IBM Certified Quantum Computation using Qiskit v2.X Developer - Associate

predecessor.title: IBM Certified Associate Developer - Quantum Computation using Qiskit v0.2X

exam.target_stack: Qiskit SDK v2.x + Qiskit Runtime primitives (SamplerV2 / EstimatorV2)

delivery.value: Pearson VUE (test center or OnVUE online proctored)

sample_test.note: Official free Sample Test linked from the exam page; optional paid Assessment Exam also exists.

provenance.section_titles: Official (IBM Advocate Guide: Qiskit v2.X Certification PDF; matches exam-page snippets). Confidence: high.
provenance.weights: Triangulated from three independent public sources (official-page search snippet, EDUSUM, ppalme review); sum = 100. Confidence: high. Not yet read verbatim off the exam page (fetch blocked); re-verify when reachable.
provenance.objectives: Official wording from IBM 'Advocate Guide: Qiskit v2.X Certification' (IBM Quantum PDF distributed to advocates; topic list mirrors the public exam page). Confidence: high.
provenance.scope_notes: Synthesized from the official 21-question sample test, first-hand exam reviews, and docs; guidance for question generation, NOT official blueprint text.
provenance.resources: Resource names verbatim from the IBM Advocate Guide; mapped to quantum.cloud.ibm.com URLs; slug existence verified against a Qiskit/documentation clone (2026-03 state). Liveness check pending in CI.
provenance.nda_note: Everything here derives from public/officially-distributed prep material. No actual exam content.

sections.title: Perform quantum operations
scope_notes: Pauli matrices and tensor products (e.g. Pauli('XZ') ordering and matrix form)
Gate effects on states incl. global phase (e.g. T gate on |1>)
Statevector / Operator construction and manipulation (qiskit.quantum_info)
Measurement probabilities from prepared states (e.g. after RY(theta))

sections.title: Visualize quantum circuits, measurements, and states
scope_notes: circuit.draw() variants incl. mpl output and conditional-circuit rendering
plot_histogram of counts
plot_bloch_multivector, plot_state_qsphere (read amplitude/phase encoding)
Statevector plots: identify state from visualization and vice versa

sections.title: Create quantum circuits
scope_notes: QuantumCircuit, QuantumRegister/ClassicalRegister, composing and measuring
Dynamic circuits: if_test / classical feedforward and control flow
Parameter / ParameterVector, assign_parameters
generate_preset_pass_manager, optimization levels 0-3, ISA circuits, transpiler stages, pass managers

resources.title: Video: Introduction to transpilation
resources.note: URL to be resolved from Qiskit YouTube channel

resource_gaps: 'Parameterized circuits' guide from the Advocate Guide has no standalone slug in the current docs; content lives in construct-circuits — verify live anchor
'Optimize for hardware' guide name has no exact slug; mapped to set-optimization + transpile-with-pass-managers

sections.title: Run quantum circuits

objectives.text: Differentiate execution modes to optimize job queuing
Run quantum circuits with a real hardware in the IBM Runtime provider

scope_notes: Execution modes: single job, session (dedicated access), batch (parallel independent jobs) — when to use which
QiskitRuntimeService, backend selection (least_busy), Session/Batch context managers
Primitives take ISA circuits (transpile first)
Open Plan vs paid plans, queuing implications
resource_gaps: 'Execute on hardware' guide name has no exact slug; mapped to run-jobs-session/run-jobs-batch + hello-world

sections.title: Use the sampler primitive

objectives.text: Set sampler primitive options such as resilience levels
Bypass runtime error mitigations and implement your own
Understand the theoretical background behind the sampler primitive

scope_notes: SamplerV2: run([isa_circuit]), PUB structure, default_shots semantics
Options: dynamical decoupling, twirling; sampler returns per-shot bitstrings (BitArray)
Array broadcasting patterns for parameter sweeps
Manual/custom mitigation when bypassing runtime defaults

resources.title: Video: An Introduction to Qiskit Runtime Primitives V2
resources.note: URL to be resolved from Qiskit YouTube channel

sections.title: Use the estimator primitive

objectives.text: Set estimator primitive options such as resilience levels
Understand the theoretical background behind the estimator primitive

scope_notes: EstimatorV2: PUB = (circuit, observables, parameter_values, precision)
Precision <-> shots relationship (halving precision quadruples shots)
resilience_level 0/1/2; Zero Noise Extrapolation (ZNE), PEC; error suppression vs mitigation
Observables: SparsePauliOp, apply_layout for ISA alignment

resources.title: Specify observables in the Pauli basis
Video: An Introduction to Qiskit Runtime Primitives V2
resources.note: URL to be resolved from Qiskit YouTube channel

sections.title: Retrieve and analyze the results of quantum circuits
scope_notes: service.job(job_id), job.result(), job.status()
Result data bins: DataBin, BitArray, get_counts()
session.details() keys (state, last-job timestamps)
Analyzing counts and expectation values

sections.title: Operate with OpenQASM

objectives.text: Interpret OpenQASM semantics for versions 2 and 3
Interoperate different versions of OpenQASM with Qiskit
Interact with the Qiskit IBM Runtime REST API

scope_notes: OpenQASM 3 classical types (incl. complex), casting rules
qiskit.qasm3.dump/dumps/load/loads; qasm2 equivalents; version semantic differences
Feature differences OpenQASM 2 vs 3 (control flow, types)
Runtime REST API basics: auth, submitting primitive jobs over REST

global_resources.title: IBM Quantum Learning: Understanding Quantum Information and Computation (John Watrous)
global_resources.note: First course of the Watrous series; theory foundation

global_resources.title: IBM Quantum Learning: Quantum Computing in Practice
Qiskit YouTube: Preparing for Qiskit Developer Certification 2.0
IBM Quantum Platform setup (run on real hardware)

advocate_context.cert_requirement: Passing C1000-179 is required to advance beyond Tier 0 (into Tier 1: recognition, swag, QAMP eligibility)
advocate_context.practice_exam_activity: Creating a Qiskit v2.x certification practice exam (min 20 multi-choice questions, public link, quality-assessed) earns 1 advocate point
